# Contributing to Chromium-Ad-Detection-Filter-Archive-Data-Repository

Thank you for your interest in contributing to the `Chromium-Ad-Detection-Filter-Archive-Data-Repository`! This repository serves as a vital historical archive of content blocking data used by the Chromium project. Your contributions help ensure the accuracy, completeness, and accessibility of this valuable dataset.

## 🤝 Code of Conduct
Please note that this project is released with a [Contributor Code of Conduct](https://github.com/chirag127/Chromium-Ad-Detection-Filter-Archive-Data-Repository/blob/main/.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating in this project, you agree to abide by its terms. We are committed to fostering an inclusive and welcoming environment for everyone.

## ✨ How to Contribute

There are several ways you can contribute to this archival project:

### 1. 🐛 Reporting Issues
If you find any discrepancies, inaccuracies, missing data points, broken links, or have suggestions for improving the organization or metadata of the archive, please open an issue.

*   **Before reporting:** Please search existing issues to see if your concern has already been addressed.
*   **How to report:** Use our [Bug Report template](https://github.com/chirag127/Chromium-Ad-Detection-Filter-Archive-Data-Repository/blob/main/.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md) to provide detailed information.

### 2. 💡 Suggesting Enhancements
We welcome suggestions for enhancing the utility and scope of this archive. This could include:

*   Proposing new categories for data classification.
*   Suggesting additional metadata fields.
*   Ideas for improved documentation or historical context.
*   Any other improvements that enhance the value of this historical dataset.
*   **How to suggest:** Open a new issue and clearly describe your enhancement idea.

### 3. 📝 Submitting Data or Documentation (Pull Requests)
If you have access to historical ad detection filter lists, related documentation, or can provide more accurate metadata for existing entries, we encourage you to submit a Pull Request.

*   **Before Submitting:**
    *   Ensure your proposed changes align with the repository's purpose as a historical archive.
    *   If your contribution involves significant new data, consider opening an issue first to discuss it with the maintainers.
*   **General Guidelines:**
    *   Fork the repository.
    *   Create a new branch for your changes (e.g., `feature/add-new-filter-list` or `fix/metadata-correction`).
    *   Make your changes, ensuring they are well-documented and follow existing data formats where applicable.
    *   Write a clear, concise [Pull Request description](https://github.com/chirag127/Chromium-Ad-Detection-Filter-Archive-Data-Repository/blob/main/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md) detailing your changes and their purpose.
    *   Ensure your branch is up-to-date with the `main` branch before submitting.

### 4. 🛡️ Security Vulnerabilities
If you discover a security vulnerability related to the integrity or accessibility of the archived data, please report it responsibly following our [Security Policy](https://github.com/chirag127/Chromium-Ad-Detection-Filter-Archive-Data-Repository/blob/main/.github/SECURITY.md).

## ✅ Contribution Checklist (for Pull Requests)

*   [ ] I have forked the repository and created my branch from `main`.
*   [ ] My changes align with the archival nature and purpose of this repository.
*   [ ] If adding new data, I have ensured its historical relevance and accuracy.
*   [ ] I have reviewed my own changes for consistency and clarity.
*   [ ] My commit messages are clear and descriptive.
*   [ ] I have linked any relevant issues in my Pull Request description.

Thank you for helping us maintain this valuable historical resource!
